name: Panel Overview (Outline)
title: Panel Overview (Outline)
intro: The panel overview of the Panel Designer lists controls which are integrated into the currently selected panel.
In the tabular overview, you can

paragraphs: All controls which you have integrated into the panel are displayed.
Only controls which you have integrated into the panel and to which a symbol can be assigned are displayed.
Integrated Group Boxes are always displayed, since they may include controls which can be linked to symbols.
This option allows you to display the fully qualified names of the assigned symbols.
tables: All controls which you have integrated into the panel are displayed.

Only controls which you have integrated into the panel and to which a symbol can be assigned are displayed. Integrated Group Boxes are always displayed, since they may include controls which can be linked to symbols.
This option allows you to display the fully qualified names of the assigned symbols.

paragraphs: Name and icon of the control.You can copy and paste a control name via the context menu. symbolizes an unknown control.
The assigned symbol as an image, e.g. for a system variable.
The symbol name of the assigned symbol.
tables: Name and icon of the control.You can copy and paste a control name via the context menu. symbolizes an unknown control.
The assigned symbol as an image, e.g. for a system variable.
The symbol name of the assigned symbol.

paragraphs: You can assign a symbol to the control in the Symbol Name column
You can sort the list by clicking the table header:
In all cases, the classification of the elements in the Group Box remains the same, the elements are sorted within the Group Box.

You can mark several controls (lines) and assign them the same symbol with a unique action (see above Assigning Symbols). The selected symbol is not assigned to controls that do not support the selected symbol type.
Below the list of the symbols, further information is displayed for the symbol selected in the list, for example the name of the database, the name of the node and the name of the message.
